attractions.name: Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (Duomo)
attractions.description: The iconic red dome dominates Florence's skyline. The intricate marble façade and sheer scale of the structure set the tone for the city's artistic grandeur
attractions.hours: Cathedral: 10:15 AM – 3:45 PM | Dome climb: 8:15 AM – 7:30 PM (Sunday Closed)
source_files: Netherlands/Anurag_Europe_All Inclusive Guide.docx

attractions.name: Piazza del Duomo
attractions.description: Street musicians, historic buildings, and the constant hum of the city make this square feel alive
source_files: Netherlands/Anurag_Europe_All Inclusive Guide.docx
Italy/Italy- Final Itinerary with booking links.pdf

attractions.name: Piazza della Signoria
attractions.description: An open-air museum filled with sculptures and historic significance. The square feels grand yet accessible
source_files: Netherlands/Anurag_Europe_All Inclusive Guide.docx
Italy/Italy- Final Itinerary with booking links.pdf

attractions.name: Palazzo Vecchio
attractions.description: A striking medieval palace that once served as Florence's political centre
source_files: Netherlands/Anurag_Europe_All Inclusive Guide.docx

attractions.name: Ponte Vecchio
attractions.description: One of Florence's most iconic bridges, lined with charming shops and offering beautiful views over the Arno River
source_files: Netherlands/Anurag_Europe_All Inclusive Guide.docx
Italy/Italy- Final Itinerary with booking links.pdf

attractions.name: Piazzale Michelangelo
attractions.description: One of the most breathtaking panoramic views of the city. As the sun begins to set, the entire city glows in warm golden hues
source_files: Netherlands/Anurag_Europe_All Inclusive Guide.docx

transport_options.description: Tram T1 connects Florence to surrounding areas including hotels
The centre of Florence is quite compact and walkable, most people get around on foot
